questions.question: The word 'Computer' is derived from the English word:
questions.explanation: Page 488: The word computer has derived from an English word 'Compute', which means 'to calculate'.

questions.question: Who is called the 'Father of Modern Computer'?
questions.explanation: Page 488: British scientist Charles Babbage was the first person to conceive an automatic calculator or a computer in 1833. He is called the 'Father of Modern Computer'.

questions.question: Blaise Pascal developed the first mechanical calculator in:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Blaise Pascal had developed the first mechanical calculator in 1642 AD, which is called 'Pascalene'.

questions.question: The credit of developing first computer program goes to:
questions.explanation: Page 488: The credit of developing first computer program goes to Lady Ada Augusta, a student of Babbage.

questions.question: Herman Holorith prepared an electronic tabulating machine in 1880 using:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Herman Holorith prepared an electronic tabulating machine in 1880, automatically functional with the help of Punch Card.

questions.question: Howard Ekin developed the first Mechanical Computer called:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Howard Ekin developed the first Mechanical Computer 'Mark-I' in 1937.

questions.question: J.P. Ekart and John Moschley invented world's first electronic computer in 1946 called:
questions.explanation: Page 488: J.P. Ekart and John Moschley invented world's first electronic computer 'ENIAC-I' in 1946.

questions.question: Electronic Valve or Vacuum Tube was used as a switch in:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Electronic Valve or Vacuum Tube was used as a switch in the first generation computer (ENIAC-I).

questions.question: John Von Newman invented EDVAC (Electronic Descrete Variable Computer) in:
questions.explanation: Page 488: John Von Newman invented EDVAC in 1951, in which he used Stored Program. The credit of using Binary System in computers also goes to him.

questions.question: The credit of using Binary System in computers goes to:
questions.explanation: Page 488: The credit of using Binary System in computers goes to John Von Newman.

questions.question: The main electronic component of First Generation (1940-52) computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: First Generation (1940-52) used Electronic Valve/Vacuum Tube. Examples: EDSAC, EDVAC, UNIVAC.

questions.question: The main electronic component of Second Generation (1952-64) computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Second Generation (1952-64) used Transistor. Examples: IBM-700, IBM-1401, IBM-1620.

questions.question: The main electronic component of Third Generation (1964-71) computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Third Generation (1964-71) used Integrated Circuit. Examples: IBM-360, IBM-370, NCR-395.

questions.question: The main electronic component of Fourth Generation (1971-) computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Fourth Generation (1971-) uses Largely Integrated Circuit. Examples: APPLE, DCM.

questions.question: Fifth Generation computers will use:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Fifth Generation uses Optical Fibre and Artificial Intelligence.

questions.question: Computers used by individual persons for domestic and official purposes are called:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Micro Computers are used by individual, also called PC or Personal Computer, for domestic and official purposes.

questions.question: Mini Computer is how many times more powerful than Micro Computer?
questions.explanation: Page 488: Mini Computer is comparatively larger and 5 to 50 times more powerful than Micro Computer.

questions.question: In Main Frame Computer, more than how many people can work at a time using Time Sharing and Multi Tasking?
questions.explanation: Page 488: Main Frame Computer: By Time Sharing and Multi Tasking techniques many people rather more than 100 people can work at a time.

questions.question: Super Computers are the most expensive and the fastest computers, able to process:

options.B: Most complex jobs with very high speed

questions.explanation: Page 488: Super Computers are very powerful, most expensive and fastest, able to process most complex jobs with very high speed.

questions.question: In Quantum Computers, Q-Bit will be used in lieu of:
questions.explanation: Page 488: In Quantum Computers Q-Bit will be used in lieu of Binary Bits.

questions.question: The first programming language (1st Generation, 1940-52) was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: 1st Generation (1940-52) programming language was FORTRAN-i.

questions.question: FORTRAN-ii, ALGOL-60, COBOL, LISP belong to which generation of programming languages?
questions.explanation: Page 488: 2nd Generation (1952-64) languages: FORTRAN-ii, ALGOL-60, COBOL, LISP.

questions.question: PL/I, ALGOL-W, ALGOL-68, Pascal, SIMULA-67, APL, SNOBOL, 4 BASIC, C belong to:
questions.explanation: Page 488: 3rd Generation (1964-71) languages include PL/I, ALGOL-W, ALGOL-68, Pascal, SIMULA-67, APL, SNOBOL, 4 BASIC, C.

questions.question: 4th Generation (1971-90) programming languages include:

options.B: CLUE, ALFARD, UCLID, Reformed Pascal, MODULA, EDA, ORACLE

questions.explanation: Page 488: 4th Generation (1971-90): CLUE, ALFARD, UCLID, Reformed Pascal, MODULA, EDA, ORACLE.

questions.question: 5th Generation (Since 1990) uses:
questions.explanation: Page 488: 5th Generation (Since 1990) uses Artificial Intelligence Languages.

questions.question: A computer is an electronic machine that helps to:

options.B: Process data and solve problems in almost all fields

questions.explanation: Page 488: A computer is an electronic machine that helps to process data and solve problems in education, home, medicine, science, technology, research, designing, publishing, communication etc.

questions.question: A computer is an information-processing and:
questions.explanation: Page 488: A computer is an information-processing and information-accessing tool.

questions.question: Information processing is the essence of:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Information processing is the essence of computing.

questions.question: Computer is an electronic device which processes the input informations according to:

options.B: Given set of instructions called program

questions.explanation: Page 488: Computer processes input informations according to the given set of instructions, called program.

questions.question: John Von Newman used which concept in EDVAC?
questions.explanation: Page 488: John Von Newman invented EDVAC in 1951, in which he used Stored Program concept.

questions.question: IBM-700, IBM-1401, IBM-1620, CDC-3600, ATLAS, ICL-1901 are examples of which generation?
questions.explanation: Page 488: These are Second Generation computers using Transistors.

questions.question: IBM-360, IBM-370, NCR-395, CDC-1700, ICL-2903 are examples of which generation?
questions.explanation: Page 488: These are Third Generation computers using Integrated Circuits.

questions.question: APPLE and DCM are examples of which generation computers?
questions.explanation: Page 488: APPLE, DCM are Fourth Generation computers using Largely Integrated Circuits.

questions.question: Desktop, Laptop, Tablets are examples of:
questions.explanation: Page 488: PCs are used for domestic and official purposes like Desktop, Laptop, Tablets etc.

questions.question: The era of computers started in which centuries?
questions.explanation: Page 488: The era of 20th and 21st century has witnessed rapid developments in science and technology.

questions.question: Computer and Information Technology (IT) in recent years has become:

options.B: An integral part of our life

questions.explanation: Page 488: Computer and Information Technology (IT) in recent years has become an integral part of our life.

questions.question: One of the greatest things that man has ever created is:
questions.explanation: Page 488: One of the greatest things that man has ever created is, perhaps, 'the Computer'.

questions.question: EDSAC, EDVAC, UNIVAC are computers of which generation?
questions.explanation: Page 488: EDSAC, EDVAC, UNIVAC are First Generation (1940-52) computers using Electronic Valve/Vacuum Tube.

questions.question: The first mechanical calculator 'Pascalene' was developed by:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Blaise Pascal developed the first mechanical calculator 'Pascalene' in 1642 AD.

questions.question: The Second Revolution of computers was given a right direction by:

options.B: EDVAC and Stored Program concept by Newman

questions.explanation: Page 488: Newman's EDVAC and Stored Program concept gave a right direction to the Computer Revolution (Second Revolution).

questions.question: Computer is used in areas of:

options.B: Administration, medicine, education, sports, defence, shops, home, markets

questions.explanation: Page 488: Computer is being used in areas of administration, medicine, education, sports, defence, shops, home, markets and many more.

questions.question: Quantum Computers are probably more advanced than:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Probably Quantum Computers will be more advanced than that of human brain.

questions.question: The development of Quantum Computer is in:
questions.explanation: Page 488: The development of Quantum Computer is in final stage.

questions.question: A computer accepts information or data from the outside world and:

options.B: Processes it to produce a new information

questions.explanation: Page 488: A computer accepts information/data from outside world, processes it to produce new information.

questions.question: The period of Third Generation computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Third Generation period was 1964-71, using Integrated Circuits.

questions.question: The period of First Generation computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: First Generation period was 1940-52.

questions.question: The period of Second Generation computers was:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Second Generation period was 1952-64.

questions.question: Herman Holorith's tabulating machine was automatically functional with the help of:
questions.explanation: Page 488: Herman Holorith's machine in 1880 was automatically functional with the help of Punch Card.

questions.question: The first electronic computer ENIAC-I paved the way for first revolution in the field of:
questions.explanation: Page 488: ENIAC-I paved the way for first revolution in the field of calculating machine or computer.

questions.question: Main Frame Computers use which techniques to allow multiple users?
questions.explanation: Page 488: Main Frame Computer: By Time Sharing and Multi Tasking techniques, many people can work at different terminals.
